description: Implement treb prune, treb reset, and treb migrate commands â€” housekeeping and maintenance commands that keep the registry and configuration in a healthy, consistent state. prune removes orphaned/invalid registry entries, reset wipes registry state with scoped targeting, and migrate handles v1â†’v2 config conversion and versioned registry schema migrations.

userStories.title: treb prune â€” Validation Infrastructure and Dry-Run
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `treb prune` to scan the deployment registry for broken cross-references and report prune candidates so that I can identify stale or orphaned entries before committing to any deletion.
acceptanceCriteria: `PruneArgs` clap struct defined with `--dry-run`, `--include-pending`, `--network`, and `--json` flags
`PruneCandidate` struct with `id`, `kind` (BrokenTransactionRef | BrokenDeploymentRef | DestroyedOnChain | OrphanedPendingEntry), `reason`, `chain_id` fields
`find_prune_candidates` detects `BrokenTransactionRef` for deployments pointing to missing transaction IDs
`find_prune_candidates` detects `BrokenDeploymentRef` for transactions referencing missing deployment IDs
`find_prune_candidates` returns empty vec for a clean registry
`chain_id` filter correctly restricts results to the matching chain
`PruneCandidate` serializes to valid JSON
`Commands::Prune` variant wired in `main.rs` (replaces stub)
Typecheck passes (`cargo check --workspace`)

userStories.title: treb prune â€” Destructive Pruning with Backup
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `treb prune` (non-dry-run) to create a timestamped backup and then remove all identified prune candidates from the registry so that I can safely clean up stale entries with an undo path.
acceptanceCriteria: `backup_registry` creates `.treb/backups/prune-<timestamp>/` and copies registry files via `snapshot_registry()`
Confirmation prompt shown before deletion (skipped with `--yes` / `-y` flag)
After destructive prune, `find_prune_candidates` on the same registry returns empty vec
`--dry-run` does not invoke `backup_registry` or modify any files
`--yes` flag skips the confirmation prompt without interactive input
Output includes table/JSON of removed entries with reasons and backup path
Typecheck passes (`cargo check --workspace`)

userStories.title: treb reset â€” Scoped Registry Reset with Backup
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `treb reset` to wipe registry state with optional `--network` and `--namespace` scoping so that I can clear a specific subset of registry entries without destroying the entire registry.
acceptanceCriteria: `ResetArgs` clap struct defined with `--network`, `--namespace`, `--yes`, and `--json` flags
Reset with `--network` only removes entries matching that chain ID across all stores
Reset with `--namespace` only removes deployments in that namespace
Reset with both `--network` and `--namespace` applies AND filter
Reset with no filters removes all entries from all stores
Backup created at `.treb/backups/reset-<timestamp>/` before any mutation
`--yes` flag skips confirmation prompt
Empty registry prints "Nothing to reset." and exits without prompting or backing up
Lookup index is empty after a full reset
`Commands::Reset` variant wired in `main.rs` (replaces stub)
Typecheck passes (`cargo check --workspace`)

userStories.title: treb migrate â€” Config Format Detection and v1â†’v2 Conversion
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `treb migrate config` to detect the treb.toml format and convert v1 (`[ns.*]`) configs to v2 (`[accounts.*]` / `[namespace.*]`) so that legacy projects can be modernized in-place with a safe backup.
acceptanceCriteria: `MigrateSubcommand` enum with `Config { dry_run, json }` and `Registry { dry_run }` variants
`run_config` with a v1 fixture file produces correct v2 TOML output (golden test)
`run_config --dry-run` prints v2 TOML to stdout without modifying any files
`run_config` with an already-v2 file prints "already v2" and exits without changes
Backup written to `treb.toml.bak-<timestamp>` before overwriting with v2 content
`run_config` with deprecated `foundry.toml` sender keys migrates them to `treb.toml`
`serialize_treb_config_v2()` added to `treb-config` crate and round-trips through `load_treb_config_v2`
`Commands::Migrate` variant wired in `main.rs` (replaces stub)
Typecheck passes (`cargo check --workspace`)

userStories.title: treb migrate registry â€” Versioned Registry Migration Runner
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `treb migrate registry` to apply forward-only versioned schema migrations to the registry so that future schema changes can be applied safely and idempotently.
acceptanceCriteria: `run_migrations(registry_dir)` function defined in `treb-registry` with static `MIGRATIONS` slice
`run_migrations` on an up-to-date registry applies zero migrations and returns `MigrationReport { applied: [], current_version: 1 }`
`run_migrations` on a `version: 0` fixture applies all pending migrations in order
Each migration step creates a backup at `.treb/backups/migrate-v{N}-<timestamp>/` before mutating
`run_registry --dry-run` lists pending migrations without calling `run_migrations` or modifying files
Registry with `version > REGISTRY_VERSION` returns a clear `TrebError::Registry` error
`MigrationReport` struct derives `Serialize` and produces valid JSON
Typecheck passes (`cargo check --workspace`)

userStories.title: Integration Tests for prune, reset, and migrate
userStories.description: As a developer, I want integration tests covering all three commands end-to-end so that the CLI behavior is verified against real fixture registries and config files.
acceptanceCriteria: `treb prune --dry-run` with a broken-ref fixture outputs candidate list and modifies no files
`treb prune --yes` with the same fixture removes the broken entry, creates backup, outputs summary
`treb prune --dry-run` on a clean registry outputs "Nothing to prune."
`treb reset --yes` on a populated registry empties all stores and creates backup
`treb reset --network <name> --yes` removes only entries for that chain ID
`treb reset --namespace default --yes` removes only entries in that namespace
`treb migrate config --dry-run` with v1 fixture: stdout matches v2 golden, no file written
`treb migrate config` with v1 fixture: `treb.toml` rewritten to v2 golden, backup file exists
`treb migrate config` with already-v2 file: no files modified, correct "already v2" message
`treb migrate registry --dry-run` on current-version registry outputs "up to date" message
All existing tests continue to pass
Typecheck passes (`cargo check --workspace`)
